AAPL.reasoning: Branch A (Fade the Extreme) fires on the hourly timeframe, with supplementary caution from daily structure. Hourly Z-score of 2.52 exceeds the +2 threshold; hourly Bollinger %B of 1.283 places price clearly outside the upper band — a statistical stretch. Hourly price (282.09) is 3.98% above the 48-period EMA and 5.76% above the 120-period SMA, with the Z-score confirming extension. Hourly relative volume is 0.59x average — declining volume on the spike is consistent with an exhaustion move rather than a breakout with institutional conviction. Daily price (271.35, last close) sits 4.03% above the 50-SMA — just below the 5% noteworthy threshold — with daily Z-score of 1.32, not extreme on the daily alone. The wiki technicals_tldr (Apr 30) identified $270–282 as the break-above target zone and flagged this level as resistance requiring post-earnings confirmation. Hourly price at 282.09 has now tagged the upper bound of that target zone, aligning with prior resistance pivot at 276.11 (daily) and the hourly Fibonacci 23.6% retrace (281.98) from the recent hourly swing — a confluence of resistance levels. Branch A entry rule requires a reversal candle or momentum shift; no confirmed reversal candle is present yet, which limits confidence. The daily RSI-14 at 61.64 shows a bullish divergence flag in the data (rising price + RSI declining over 60 bars), which contradicts the fade thesis and is a key risk. Branch B (buy the dip) does not fire — price is extended above all moving averages with no pullback to a Fibonacci level. Confidence capped at 52: the hourly extension and resistance confluence support a fade, but the daily trend remains intact (EMAs aligned up, ADX plus_di 32.51 vs minus_di 18.67), earnings appear to have resolved bullish (gap up to 282), and the recent NVDA dip-buy lesson (stop at Fib 61.8%) reminds us that counter-trend fades in strong uptrends carry elevated stop-out risk. Entry requires patience for a reversal signal at current levels; target is mean reversion to daily 50-SMA (260.83) or at minimum the hourly middle Bollinger Band (271.40). Stop placed above the recent hourly high of 287.21.
